theme_notes: Drawn from: Iran's 14-point peace proposal as the US-Iran war hits day 61 (Trump ordering naval escorts through the Strait of Hormuz, gas at $4.45/gal), PSG's ludicrous 5-4 Champions League semifinal win over Bayern Munich in Paris, and The Devil Wears Prada sequel premiering May 1 with Meryl Streep and Anne Hathaway reuniting.
settled: Balanced. Iran's 14-point peace proposal isn't. Cherish this.
Square. PSG and Bayern played to a 5-4 first leg. You two ended calmer.
Nothing owed. Meryl Streep got a sequel. Some things actually work out.
youOwe: Less than a gallon of gas at $4.45. The embarrassment is priced correctly.
Bayern conceded five in Paris and still has to play a second leg. You can handle $30.
Smaller than the filing fee on Spirit's bankruptcy. Silver linings.
The Devil Wears Prada didn't need a sequel. This debt didn't need to exist.
Iran submitted 14 demands. Your partner submitted one. Still unanswered.
Trump's approval hit a record low. Your partner's goodwill is trending the same.
At $4.45 a gallon, this is 67 gallons of accumulated regret.
PSG scored 5 goals in one first leg. This debt survived longer than Bayern's composure.
Meryl Streep came back for the sequel. You haven't come back with the money.
The Strait of Hormuz is blockaded. So is any goodwill you had left.
The Iran war is 61 days old. This debt is younger and somehow worse.
The Devil Wears Prada sequel premiered with better reviews than your excuses.
The US is now escorting ships through the Strait of Hormuz. You are a geopolitical incident.
Gas hit $4.45 because of a war. What's your excuse for this number?
Iran wants 14 concessions. Your partner wants one payment. Iran is negotiating harder.
Iran's 14-point peace proposal had better terms than whatever you're planning.
The US deployed warships to Hormuz for less provocation than this debt represents.
The Devil Wears Prada got a sequel. Your relationship might not.
theyOwe: One gallon of gas at current war-economy prices. Mortifying for everyone.
PSG scored five goals in 90 minutes. You can ask for $20 in 20 seconds.
Smaller than the print cost of Iran's 14-point peace proposal. Still real.
Chase this with the energy the US brings to the Strait of Hormuz. Relentlessly.
The Devil Wears Prada got a sequel decades later. You deserve $100 this week.
Iran submitted 14 demands. You submitted one. Time to follow up.
At $4.45 a gallon, this is a lot of tank-filling distance you could put between you.
Iran's ceasefire talks have more structure than your partner's payment timeline.
Bayern conceded five and still showed up in Munich. Take notes.
The Strait of Hormuz standoff is 61 days old. This balance is older.
PSG dismantled Bayern 5-4 in Paris last Tuesday. This debt outlasted the news cycle.
Record-low approval ratings are bad. Being owed $700 is a different kind of bad.
The US is escorting ships through Hormuz for less reason. Consider your options.
Meryl Streep came back for a Devil Wears Prada sequel. Your $3k hasn't come back for anything.
Gas costs $4.45 because Iran controls a bottleneck. Your money has its own bottleneck.
Iran's been at war for 61 days. This debt is older. Someone needs to broker peace.
The US is escorting ships through the Strait of Hormuz. No one escorts your money.
Iran offered 14 concessions. Your partner has offered zero. Iran is working harder on it.
